mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls over the prior three months signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between 3-month PAYEMS change and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean forward return in top PAYEMS quintiles is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% restricts liquidity and raises discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as borrowing costs squeeze margins and reduce buyback activity.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 50% of subsequent months, or the mean return in those months is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong retail sales and low unemployment confirm that consumption growth is income-funded rather than debt-funded, amplifying the positive link between demand strength and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to improve prediction over RSAFS alone with incremental R-squared below 0.01, or the joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during early-cycle recoveries when prior economic slack allows demand to absorb supply without immediate inflationary bottlenecks.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month acceleration during early-cycle recovery periods coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, SPX 20-day returns are positive in greater than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and forward construction activity, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 3-month change in HOUST shows a Rank IC below 0.02 with 5-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive HOUST momentum coincides with negative mean forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing four-quarter trend signals persistent demand momentum that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth surprises relative to trend produce a Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day SPX returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5% over two months confirms a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate revenue and profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 1.5% over two months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of instances, or the average forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).
